title: Starting the Uniface Debugger

Starting the Uniface Debugger

Starting the Uniface Debugger

You can start a debugging session with the Uniface Debugger in several ways.

  • Enter the debug statement in ProcScript where you want to start debugging. The Debugger starts whenever the debug statement is encountered.
    Tip: In a desktop application, you could put the debug statement in the rarely-used menu trigger, which enables you to start the Debugger by pressing Gold+C.

    Component

    Location

    Effect

    Application shell

    First line of apStart trigger

    Debugger starts as soon as the application begins and takes control of execution.

    Application shell

    First line of keyboardSwitch trigger

    Start the application and enter GOLD+Y to start the Debugger.

    Application shell

    First line of menu trigger

    Start the application and enter GOLD+C to start the Debugger.

    Any component

    Any ProcScript module

    Debugger starts whenever debug is reached.
    CAUTION: Ensure that you remove any debug statements before deploying the application in a production environment.

  • Use the /deb command line switch in either the application’s shortcut target, or on the application’s command line to start an application or component in debug mode.

    Both the Debugger and the application start automatically. The application stops on the first ProcScript statement and the Debugger takes control of ProcScript execution.

    You can optionally specify the name of the component you want to debug. For example, to test and debug a specific form in the development environment, start the IDE and enter /deb /tst FormName.

  • Configure the Uniface Debugger to connect to a local or remote Uniface Server over a TCP or TLS network connection. This is needed for debugging components such as services and server pages, which execute in a Uniface Server process rather than a local client.

    For more information, see Debugging Remote and Server Processes and Debugging a Web Application.

    Note: Uniface provides a pre-configured environment for remote debugging over TCP, with default shortcuts for the Uniface Debugger in the Uniface program group.
Related Links
  • Controlling Code Execution in the Debugger

    Debugger execution control commands allow the application to run through ProcScript that is not of interest (under varying degrees of control), and then return control to the Debugger.

  • Debugging Remote and Server Processes

    To debug components that run in a different process than the client application, the Debugger and the application being debugged communicate with each other over a network. For this reason, it is often referred to as remote debugging, even if both the Debugger and application process run on the same local machine. The network protocol can be TCP or TLS.

  • Debugging a Web Application

    Debugging a web application requires the use of remote debugging, in which the Uniface Debugger and the Uniface Web Application Server communicate with each other over a network. They can be running on the same machine or on different machines, and the network protocol can be TCP or TLS. Either one can be started first, but their configurations must match.

  • Debugging in Character Mode

    On a client running in character mode, the debug ProcScript statement causes a Debug command line to be displayed at the bottom of the screen.

Was this topic helpful?
LikeDislike
